Free UK shipping 90-day guarantee Secure payment

Product Details

Taste & Aroma

Our PCOS tea offers a naturally refreshing and slightly minty flavor with warm, earthy undertones. The spearmint provides a cooling, crisp taste that's balanced by the subtle sweetness of cinnamon and jujube.

Flavor Profile:

  • Top notes: Fresh spearmint with a hint of sweetness
  • Mid notes: Warm cinnamon and earthy nettle
  • Base notes: Gentle ginger spice with grounding ashwagandha

The aroma is soothing and inviting - think cozy mornings with a touch of herbal freshness. It's naturally caffeine-free and has a smooth, clean finish that makes it perfect for any time of day.

What to expect: Not too sweet, not too bitter - just a perfectly balanced herbal tea that you'll actually look forward to drinking daily. The ginger adds a subtle warmth without overpowering, while the mulberry leaf and jujube round out the flavor with a delicate, natural sweetness.

Best enjoyed hot, but equally delicious iced for those warm days ☀️

Sourcing & Production

Quality you can trust, crafted with centuries-old wisdom 🌿

We partnered with a small, family-owned farm in the Sichuan region of China - an area renowned for its rich soil and ideal climate for growing medicinal herbs. Unlike mass producers, they own their land and have been cultivating herbs using traditional methods passed down through generations.

Why we chose them:

After testing over a dozen suppliers for more than a year, this partnership stood out. Their commitment to quality isn't just about business - it's personal. They source the finest organic herbs from across the country, selecting only plants at peak potency.

What makes it special:

Our producer uses ancient preservation techniques that protect the delicate flavors and active compounds of each plant. No shortcuts, no harsh processing - just time-honored methods that ensure you get the full benefits nature intended.

Every batch is small-batch production, carefully crafted to maintain the highest standards. From soil to cup, every step is intentional.

Our promise:

We spent a year finding the right partner because your health deserves nothing less. This isn't just tea - it's a carefully curated blend made by artisans who understand the power of plants.

Ethically sourced. Traditionally crafted. Made for you. ✨

International Shipping

We're excited to deliver your PCOS wellness journey internationally! Our tea ships to multiple countries - you'll see the full list of available destinations at checkout.

Shipping Details:

  • UK: FREE shipping ✨
  • International: Shipping fees calculated at checkout
  • Delivery time: 4-12 business days depending on your location

Important to note:

All orders pass through customs as part of the international shipping process. The good news? You won't be charged any additional customs fees - we've got that covered. However, customs processing may affect delivery times, so please allow for potential delays during busy periods.

Questions?

Track your order using the tracking number provided in your shipping confirmation email. For any shipping concerns, reach out to our support team - we're here to help! 💗

📖 How to Use:

Take 2 capsules daily

Preferably in the morning with a glass of water

Take with meals for better absorption

Recommended 3-month course for optimal results

settings.title: SPEARMINT
settings.subtitle: KEY FONCTION
settings.sidebar_first_text: A refreshing Mediterranean herb known for its cooling flavor and potent medicinal oils.
settings.sidebar_second_text: Clinical studies show that spearmint tea can significantly lower "free" testosterone levels. This helps reduce androgen-related symptoms such as hirsutism (unwanted hair growth), hormonal acne, and thinning hair.

settings.title: Nettle
settings.subtitle: KEY FONCTION
settings.sidebar_first_text: A nutrient-dense wild plant packed with iron, minerals, and anti-inflammatory compounds.
settings.sidebar_second_text: Nettle works by binding to SHBG (Sex Hormone Binding Globulin) to help regulate the amount of free testosterone in the blood. It also helps stabilize blood sugar levels and improves the body's response to insulin.

settings.title: Cinnamon
settings.subtitle: KEY FONCTION
settings.sidebar_first_text: A warm, aromatic spice derived from the inner bark of Cinnamomum trees.
settings.sidebar_second_text: Cinnamon is a powerful "insulin sensitizer." It mimics insulin’s effects and improves glucose transport into cells, which helps regulate the menstrual cycle and reduces the metabolic "spikes" that trigger PCOS symptoms.

settings.title: Ashwagandha
settings.subtitle: KEY FONCTION
settings.sidebar_first_text: A premier adaptogenic root used for centuries in Ayurvedic medicine to help the body manage stress.
settings.sidebar_second_text: By lowering cortisol (the stress hormone), Ashwagandha helps prevent "adrenal" PCOS flares. It also supports the balance of FSH and LH, the hormones responsible for regular ovulation.

settings.title: Mulberry Leaf
settings.subtitle: KEY FONCTION
settings.sidebar_first_text: The leaf of the Mulberry tree, rich in a unique compound called DNJ.
settings.sidebar_second_text: DNJ inhibits the enzymes that break down carbohydrates into sugar. Drinking this before or with a meal can reduce glucose and insulin spikes by up to 40%, helping to manage the insulin resistance at the root of PCOS.

settings.title: Jujube (Red Date)
settings.subtitle: KEY FONCTION
settings.sidebar_first_text: A nutrient-rich fruit often called the "Red Date," prized in Eastern medicine for its calming properties.
settings.sidebar_second_text: High in flavonoids and Vitamin C, Jujube fights the chronic low-grade inflammation associated with PCOS. It also supports better sleep quality, which is essential for healthy hormone production and weight management.

settings.title: Ginger
settings.subtitle: KEY FONCTION
settings.sidebar_first_text: Ginger acts as a natural COX-2 inhibitor, reducing the inflammation that causes painful periods. Research suggests it also helps balance reproductive hormones and may improve the number of healthy follicles in the ovaries.
settings.sidebar_second_text: Ginger acts as a natural COX-2 inhibitor, reducing the inflammation that causes painful periods. Research suggests it also helps balance reproductive hormones and may improve the number of healthy follicles in the ovaries.

settings.title: Who can consume this brew?
settings.text: This Brew is ideal for women experiencing hormonal imbalances at any life stage, including those with irregular periods, scanty flow, PCOS/PCOD, thyroid issues, perimenopause, or menopause.

settings.title: What’s the best time to drink the brew?
settings.text: The best time to take the brew is first thing in the morning and before sleeping. You can also take it alongside your breakfast.

settings.title: How long does it take to see results?
settings.text: It takes 3–6 months to see results alongside lifestyle changes.

settings.title: How many cups can I consume in a day?
Can pregnant women consume it?
settings.text: We do not recommend taking it during pregnancy.

settings.title: What happens once I stop taking the brew, will my symptoms resurface?
settings.text: Your symptoms should not resurface provided you are also following a healthy lifestyle.
